findings: GraphSpec: 0 hallucinated references across all 3 tasks
Functions: 0 hallucinated references (improvement over batch 1 — simpler tasks?)
Task 5 (fan-in): GraphSpec naturally reactive, functions treatment produced one-shot (not reactive) — strongest case for GraphReFly
Task 4 (fan-out): both treatments had bugs — GraphSpec missing filter, functions had mutable globals + separate cron
Task 6 (error-handling): GraphSpec exposed catalog gap — retry fn has nothing to retry. Actionable: catalog needs retrySource or retry.config.fn
Functions produced more complete error-handling code (retry loop, backoff, logging all inline)
GraphSpec bugs are structural and visible in topology; functions bugs are behavioral and hidden in logic
catalog_feedback: retry fn needs config.fn or config.url to know what to retry — current catalog is ambiguous
Need a filterByCategory fn or filterBy with eq op for fan-out routing (classify → filter → effect)
Feedback loops (L1-Task 4) are not expressible with current catalog — need writeBack or state-update mechanism
summary.g0_direction: positive overall — GraphSpec wins on structure visibility and reactivity; functions win on error-handling completeness
